mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending power, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 65% of instances when UNRATE rises > 0.5pp in 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects strengthening supply-side capacity and corporate output growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration yields negative or flat 5-day forward returns during late-cycle expansion periods over a 2-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation triggers expectations of tighter monetary policy and margin compression, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL MoM changes above 0.4% are followed by positive 20-day returns in > 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment rates confirms robust labor market tightening and wage growth, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence signal shows a Rank IC below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth reflects stronger aggregate economic activity and corporate earnings momentum, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show no statistically significant positive correlation with 5-day forward returns over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in industrial production signals immediate supply-side disruption and earnings downgrade risk, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO monthly declines below -0.5% are followed by positive 20-day returns in > 55% of observed cases.
